finding: A single accepted randomized acoustic codeword should not be upgraded into repeated evidence. Multiple independently randomized trials can be evaluated against the declared random exact-match probability with an exact binomial upper-tail test, while failing closed on malformed records or any environmental-confound classification.

primary_sources.use: browser-side scheduling and rendering model for independently generated acoustic challenges

primary_sources.status: W3C First Public Working Draft; work in progress
primary_sources.use: cryptographically strong random challenge material through getRandomValues

primary_sources.status: peer-reviewed-methodology preprint reference; not a web platform specification
primary_sources.use: documents conservative exact binomial confidence methods and their tradeoffs

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit source

assumptions: the null exact-match probability is correctly specified
trial outcomes are not selectively omitted
microphone and classifier behavior are stable enough across trials for comparison
falsification_route: Inject an environmental-confound trial, malformed record, insufficient trial count, or a success pattern whose exact upper-tail probability exceeds alpha; the evaluator must refuse repeated-evidence acceptance.
privacy_boundary: Store only derived trial classifications, counts, declared null probability, p-value, and challenge metadata. Do not persist raw microphone audio without separate consent and governance.
limits: No browser or iPhone Safari execution was performed.
A low p-value does not prove conscious audibility or exclusive browser causation.
Independence and null-probability assumptions are not verified by this module.
Repeated trials can be biased by selective retention unless the trial ledger is append-only and complete.
